_note: Outdoor feed: ski Dec-Apr, backpack May-Oct. Western US focus. Refreshed every ~3 weeks by GHA cron.

items.headline: CDT 2026: New Mexico National Defense Area Restricts Southern Terminus Access
items.snark: The iconic obelisk is now behind an Army fence and you need Fort Huachuca paperwork to touch it. Not the wildest way to start the divide.

items.headline: Gila Wilderness Stage 1 Fire Restrictions Begin March 23
items.snark: Fire restrictions are rolling in early this year in the Gila. Plan your cook system and adjust accordingly if you're heading down there this spring.

items.headline: Wind River High Route: The Complete 97-Mile Alpine Traverse
items.snark: Sixty-five miles off-trail, two 13ers, nine alpine passes, and Wyoming's biggest glaciers. This is the Winds at full tilt and it's not messing around.

items.headline: Hiking Trails in the Gila Wilderness: Day Hikes to the Centennial Loop
items.snark: The 100-mile Gila Centennial Loop is the full send, but there are solid day options off the Cliff Dwellings if you want a taste without the commitment.

items.headline: Continental Divide Trail Hiking Advice from the Class of 2025
items.snark: Colorado is hard no matter how strong you feel coming out of New Mexico, says the Class of 2025. The San Juans will humble anyone, every time.

items.headline: Backpacking a CDT Sampler in Colorado's Weminuche Wilderness
items.snark: Thirty miles of CDT through the Weminuche with sustained time above 12,000 feet. This is Colorado showing off and you get a front-row seat.

items.headline: 5 Reasons You Must Backpack the Wind River Range
items.snark: No permits, alpine lakes you'll lose count of, and fewer tourists than the Tetons an hour north. The Winds are the move when Grand Teton lottery burns you.

items.headline: Best Backpacking Packs for Thru-Hiking in 2026
items.snark: Hyperlite Southwest gets the highest satisfaction rating again. If it's still working after 2,000 miles, maybe stop overthinking your pack choice.

items.headline: The Best New Gear of 2025 According to Backpacker's Gear Editor
items.snark: Durston Iceline Poles at 4.7 ounces each are the new ultralight benchmark. Also, dehydrated drunken noodles are apparently a thing now and I'm here for it.

items.headline: 14 Epic Routes for Backpacking in Colorado with Local Tips
items.snark: Ben Tyler Trail in Lost Creek opens early season when the high country is still locked up. April and May itches get scratched at 11.8 miles through the burn.

items.headline: Weminuche Wilderness: Colorado's Largest at 499,771 Acres
items.snark: Three fourteeners, hundreds of trail miles, and the Continental Divide slicing right through the heart. The Weminuche is where you go to get properly lost.

items.headline: How to Thru-Hike the PCT in 2026: Lessons from Last Year's Class
items.snark: Get your shoe and sock game figured out before the trail and stick to it at all costs. This is the wisdom that only 2,650 miles of blisters can teach.
